ACT — Law & Order policy (current, from act.org.nz/law-order)

Headline: Restoring Law & Order. ACT believes protecting the safety and property of citizens is the government's first and most important job. The rights of victims should trump the rights of criminals.

Our Values: The Government's first job is stopping crime. People who work, save and sacrifice shouldn't live in fear that someone will hurt them or take what they've worked for. It's about preserving the values of society — protecting each person's dignity, and creating things yourself and trading rather than taking by force.

Achievements ACT cites: Bringing back Three Strikes — second violent/sexual offence means no parole and full sentence, third means maximum sentence, to take the worst offenders out of circulation. Ending taxpayer-funded cultural reports (was ~$6m/year for reports pleading for lighter sentences) to put victims at the centre. Scrapping Labour's prisoner-reduction target and expanding prison capacity. Introducing new aggravating factors at sentencing for assaults on sole-charge workers or those working from home (longer sentences for targeting vulnerable shop staff, bus drivers). Strengthening police powers to target gangs and organised crime.

Next goals: Prisoner rehabilitation to save costs and reduce crime — early release for prisoners who learn to read, take on a trade, or get a qualification (a prisoner with the usual minimum non-parole period would not be eligible for parole unless they upskill). Ensure sentencing reflects community safety — keep dangerous offenders in prison while they pose a threat; parole based on evidence of rehabilitation, not political targets. Focus police resources on frontline crime-fighting — target gangs, violent offenders and repeat criminals, cut paperwork that keeps officers off the street.
